authority_boundary: Plain-language comprehension layer over the accepted public runtime organ registry. Each gloss is distilled from the organ's paper module and adversarially verified against its claim ceiling; glosses are projection metadata, not source authority.
anti_claim: Human/agent glosses, first commands, family labels, and standalone/wired notes are navigation and comprehension metadata only. They do not encode evidence strength (see core/organ_evidence_classes.json), score-based progress, maturity, readiness, product completeness, release, private-root equivalence, proof correctness, provider-call, secret-export, or whole-system-correctness authority.

generation.verified_against: each organ's paper module plus its registry claim ceiling
generation.do_not_hand_edit: Glosses are curated substrate; refine deliberately. Rebuild ORGANS.md/ARCHITECTURE.md with: PYTHONPATH=src python3 scripts/build_organ_atlas.py --write

organs.human_gloss: It checks that each declared "pattern" (a reusable bit of system structure) is properly hooked up: it has the required fields, points at real source material by reference, names the rule it answers to and what it explicitly does NOT claim, has no duplicate pattern IDs, and leaks no secrets or private bodies into the public record. A cold reader can run it to get a written receipt showing the overall pass/fail, which pattern rows are accepted versus rejected and why, and what each row is forbidden from claiming.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to validate that public pattern rows meet the declared binding contract (required fields, no duplicate pattern IDs, resolved reference capsules, no private-body or secret leakage, no public-leaf overclaim) and to read the pass/reject findings and per-row anti-claims from the emitted JSON receipt.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public pattern-binding/route-readiness contract; it does not certify the private pattern ledger, public release or hosted-public readiness, publication, provider calls, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness, and it does not turn any mined pattern row into a standalone public leaf (selection stays organ-first and fixture-bound).
organs.wiring_note: consumes the route-readiness selector contract via pattern_route_readiness (row-to-organ router, route cards, decision matrix, dependency DAG, macro validation report) and imports the real pattern ledger + substrate bindings, atop the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine

organs.human_gloss: It checks that a folder of example "doctrine" files (toy public standards and write-ups that describe how the system is supposed to behave) actually have the required parts: a stated purpose, the rule that governs them, the receipts they are expected to produce, and an honest statement of what they do NOT claim. A cold reader can see, file by file, which entries are well-formed and which ones fail a required check, including ones that overclaim (saying a passing grammar check proves the doctrine is complete) or that try to treat plain advice as enforceable authority.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs the `validate` subcommand to confirm a folder of toy public standards and paper-module fixtures has the required structure (teleology, governing standard, receipt expectations, anti-claim) with no duplicate slugs, no prose row claiming executable authority, and no doctrine-completeness overclaims; it emits per-file pass/fail receipts. A separate `validate-executable-grammar-metabolism-bundle` subcommand (not the command shown here) additionally checks that copied non-secret macro bodies match their declared SHA-256 digests via a source-module manifest.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates the declared public contract and structure of toy standards/paper-module fixtures only (the `validate` command checks shape, slugs, and overclaim/authority guards; a separate bundle command verifies copied-body digests). It does not publish macro doctrine bodies, prove doctrine completeness, export a private standards engine, authorize later organs, or claim provider calls, private-data equivalence, release readiness, or whole-system correctness.
organs.wiring_note: Reads standards/paper-module fixtures and digest-verified copied source-module bodies; binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (receipts, private_state_scan, schemas), not to other named organs.

organs.human_gloss: This organ is an evidence checkpoint that sits in front of formal-proof work. It reads the diagnostic records left by earlier proof-pipeline steps and writes a "diagnostic board" listing which checks were accepted, which were rejected, and why. A cold reader can open that board to see exactly what evidence was kept, and can watch it refuse to let raw model output, a stale record, or a merely-passing check get inflated into a claim that the math is actually correct. It only organizes and judges existing records; it never runs a proof checker itself.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to turn upstream Ring2 proof-pipeline receipt references (failure-taxonomy, graph-update, verifier-trace repair, evidence-cell anchor) into accepted/rejected diagnostic receipts, and to confirm forbidden cases (provider proof bodies, source-authority upgrades, stale receipt coupling, runtime-correctness overclaims) are rejected as regression guards before any downstream proof-witness step. It emits a diagnostic board, proof receipts, a provider-payload policy result, and a proof-evidence validation receipt over receipt refs only.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It records proof/evidence diagnostics over existing receipt references only. It does not run Lean, call providers, expose proof bodies, turn a passing check into formal-proof or theorem authority, prove runtime or whole-system correctness, authorize later organs, certify public release, authorize publication or recipient work, or establish secret export.
organs.wiring_note: Consumes upstream receipts from formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop, formal_evidence_cell_anchor_resolver, and Ring2 failure-taxonomy/graph-update runs, and feeds the diagnostic board downstream toward formal_math_lean_proof_witness and the pattern-binding/doctrine-grammar boundary.

organs.human_gloss: Before anyone tries to prove a math theorem with the Lean prover, this gate reads simple description files that declare what a math setup is supposed to have — which math library is claimed to be present, which proof tactics are reported as already probed, which lemmas may be looked up, and which limits apply to the text budgets handed to AI providers — and writes a plain checklist of what is allowed to be attempted versus blocked. It works only from those declared description files; it does not inspect the real toolchain or run anything. It matters because it makes the system's claims honest and inspectable: it refuses to let a library be marked available unless a probe result backs that up, blocks routing a proof tactic that was not probed, and refuses to let any real proof text sneak into the lemma-lookup tables or provider budgets.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to get a machine-readable readiness board listing available vs. blocked tactics, allowed lemma-lookup rows, and admissible target-shape routes before deciding what a downstream prover step may safely attempt. The board is derived from declared metadata only, so treat it as a permission/boundary surface, not as evidence the environment actually works.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only validates and projects declared readiness metadata; it does not run Lean/Lake, inspect the real toolchain, call providers, prove any theorem correct, produce benchmark scores, or authorize Mathlib-dependent proof attempts.
organs.wiring_note: consumes the macro intake cell formal_math_readiness_extensions and gates the still-bounded formal_math_lean_proof_witness (its next_boundary), atop the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine

organs.human_gloss: It reads a recorded readiness report from a Lean math toolchain run and makes one fact inspectable: when the report was captured, the Mathlib library was not importable (its import probe failed). From that, it lists which math corpora are absent or usable only for translation smoke tests, and which downstream tasks must be blocked before any Mathlib-dependent proof work is attempted. It also re-checks that the recorded source files match their recorded SHA-256 digests and that no proof bodies, provider outputs, or private paths leaked into the public output. A cold reader cares because it shows exactly where the proof pipeline draws a hard "not ready, do not proceed" line, with provenance, instead of quietly assuming the environment is fine.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to check, against copied non-secret readiness rows, whether the recorded Mathlib import probe passed and therefore which consumer cases are allowed versus blocked before routing proof or premise work. It validates and projects the recorded accounting only; it does not itself import Mathlib, run Lean, or prove anything.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only projects and gate-checks recorded corpus/toolchain readiness accounting and re-verifies recorded source digests and leakage guards. It does not run Lean or Lake, prove theorem correctness, claim Mathlib is available beyond the recorded failed probe, benchmark corpora, score model performance, call providers, or authorize release or publication.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret_exclusion_scan, receipts, schemas); it reads copied source artifacts and anchors tactic-portfolio-availability receipts as inputs/digests rather than consuming a live organ feed

organs.human_gloss: Before any proof is attempted, this organ looks at a math problem's feature tags and writes down its first-guess strategy (for example, "this looks like an if-and-only-if, so split it both ways"), and flags anything it cannot map as an explicit "no strategy matched" instead of a silent failure. The chosen opening move, why it was chosen, and the cases it could not map are all recorded in machine-readable receipts a cold reader can inspect.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to deterministically pick a pre-proof strategy hypothesis and retrieval terms from problem feature tags (highest feature overlap wins; zero overlap becomes a typed strategy-selection miss), and to check that unknown strategy ids, proof bodies, oracle labels, post-oracle selection, and release/proof/provider overclaims are all rejected.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only projects pre-oracle strategy-hypothesis and retrieval mechanics; it does not run Lean/Lake, prove theorems, establish domain or theorem correctness, reveal oracle labels, expose proof bodies, call providers, tune on test answers, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas) plus its own copied-body source manifest; no other named organ feeds or consumes it.

organs.human_gloss: It turns one captured Lean run's results into an inspectable list of which proof shortcuts ("tactics" like `rfl`, `simp`, `omega`, `aesop`) were recorded as compiling, so a reader can see at a glance which are usable and which are off before anything treats a tactic as available. In this fixture seven tactics are marked usable and `aesop` is marked failed (its recorded run hit a missing-Mathlib error). The tool reads pre-recorded status rows and checks them for honesty; it does not run Lean itself.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent reads it to get an environment-scoped map of which tactics were recorded as compiling, and relies on its checks: every tactic must carry a compile status, no Mathlib-dependent tactic may be marked available unless the Mathlib import probe passed, and any tactic a consumer references must exist in the probed portfolio.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only projects and validates which tactics were recorded as compiling in one captured environment; it does not run Lean/Lake at all, prove any goal, certify domain correctness, call providers, claim benchmark performance, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: supplies the callability map that gates downstream tactic routing, anchoring the `target_shape_tactic_routing_gate` receipts so consumers reference only probed tactics

organs.human_gloss: Before a proof is attempted, this organ checks a list of candidate proof tactics for a given goal and writes down an allow-or-reject decision, with a plain reason, for each one. Rejections fall into three kinds: the tactic isn't actually available in the declared environment, it was never listed in the environment's tested set of tactics, or it simply doesn't fit the kind of goal being proved. A cold reader can open the resulting record and see, tactic by tactic, exactly what was admitted or blocked and why, instead of trusting an opaque "we tried these" claim. It only inspects and records the routing decision over references that already exist; it never runs a prover or proves anything itself.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to produce an auditable per-tactic decision record (admissible / unavailable / unprobed / shape-inadmissible, each with a reason) over real Ring2 route references, asserting the decision is made before any proof execution. It is a checker/projector of the pre-execution routing decision, not a live prover gate: it reads supplied route refs and emits allow/reject decisions plus authority-ceiling and secret-exclusion receipts; it does not run Lean/Lake or attempt a proof.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only inspects and records the projection mechanics of pre-execution tactic-routing references — emitting per-tactic allow/reject decisions with reasons. It does not run Lean/Lake, does not prove or judge the correctness of any goal, emits no proof bodies, makes no provider calls, performs no post-execution route selection, reports no benchmark scores or maturity, and authorizes no release.
organs.wiring_note: consumes/anchors against receipts of formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop, formal_evidence_cell_anchor_resolver, and proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine over the same Ring2 run

organs.human_gloss: Lets you inspect a small, fixed catalog of Lean standard-library "premises" (named building blocks like facts about numbers, booleans, lists, and basic logic) along with the labels and source references that say where each one comes from. A cold reader can see what proof ingredients are on the table and confirm they were copied from public Lean sources, with no hidden proof text, no Mathlib, and nothing that secretly gives away test answers. It only checks and displays this catalog; it does not run Lean or prove anything.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to validate that a premise-index bundle's metadata (and, in exported-bundle mode, copied public-safe macro receipt bodies) stays inside the closed-index public-safe boundary (no Mathlib refs, no proof bodies, no oracle-needed ids, no test-split-tuning authority, every row citing an Init/ Lean source) and to read the resulting pass/blocked receipt. It never runs Lean/Lake or authorizes release.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only validates the public-safe projection of premise metadata and copied non-secret macro bodies; it does not run Lean or Lake, prove any theorem correct, expose proof bodies or oracle-needed ids, call providers, produce benchmark scores, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private-state scan, receipts, schemas); it cites the private formal_math_premise_retrieval macro run as its projection source, not as a runtime-wired organ

organs.human_gloss: Given a small copied set of Lean/Std math-lemma descriptions plus some search queries, this organ shows which lemmas a plain term-matching search would surface for each query, how it keeps each assembled context within a fixed size budget, and that it never exposes proof text or "answer-key" hints (the premise ids a solver would only get to see after the fact). Running it on the bundled first-wave fixture, a cold reader can open the receipt and see the retrieval mechanism working in miniature alongside deliberate bad inputs (a leaked proof body, leaked answer-key ids, a budget overflow, an attempt to peek at test answers, and an unknown strategy) that the organ catches, proving the leak and budget guards actually fire.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs the first-wave fixture (`run` mode, which includes the negative inputs) to replay term-scored premise retrieval over the copied index and confirm that all five guards — proof-body leakage, oracle/answer-key ids, context-budget overflow, test-split tuning, and unknown-strategy-id — observe and block their planted negative cases (expected == observed, none missing). The separate `run-retrieval-bundle` mode replays only the positive retrieval demo and does not exercise the negative cases.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only checks that public retrieval metadata is internally coherent, term-scored over a copied index, budget-bounded, and leakage-clean; it does not run Lean/Lake, call providers, prove any theorem or its own correctness, claim benchmark performance, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: hands the downstream proof-attempt boundary to the sibling organ formal_math_lean_proof_witness, which owns the bounded public witness; retrieval itself still claims no proof authority

organs.human_gloss: It replays how a proof-lab turns a verifier's failure feedback into a teaching signal, using copied (non-secret) run data so a cold reader can see the failure categories, the repair action tied to each failure, and the rule that nothing gets promoted without a fresh re-run receipt. You can inspect the whole correction loop without ever seeing actual proofs, answer keys, or model outputs, because those are deliberately kept out.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to validate that a verifier-trace repair loop over copied non-secret Ring2 run rows obeys its gates (a trace grade requires recorded trace events, every repair action must name a verifier failure class, and promotion requires a cold-rerun receipt reference) and that it rejects proof bodies, oracle-needed premise ids, provider payload bodies, and human approval treated as proof correctness.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It demonstrates control-loop projection mechanics over copied non-secret Ring2 run rows only; it does not run Lean/Lake, call providers, expose proof bodies or oracle premise ids, treat human or provider advice as correctness, prove any theorem, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (consuming copied Ring2 run substrate); the paper module only narratively positions it "between" sibling readiness/premise-retrieval/tactic-routing/diagnostic/Lean-witness surfaces, not feeding or consuming their outputs.

organs.human_gloss: When the project's writeups make proof-flavored claims about its formal-math work, this organ checks each claim against a named piece of recorded evidence and the public reference files in the repo, confirms the claim is no stronger than that evidence allows, and flags claims that have no backing or that overreach. A cold reader can see which claims are anchored to evidence and which are just words, while proof contents and any private file references are kept out of the output.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate that every formal-math claim resolves to a known evidence cell with public source-anchor refs and a permitted claim strength, that the copied macro source-module bodies match their recorded digests, and that the authority ceiling stays at metadata-only (no theorem correctness, Lean/Lake, providers, or release). The cited run-anchor-bundle command validates the clean exported bundle; running the organ's fixture mode (the `run` subcommand) instead is what exercises and confirms all seven leakage/overclaim refusal cases.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates claim-to-evidence anchoring mechanics only: claim-to-cell resolution, source-anchor presence, permitted claim strength, copied-source-module digest checks, and leakage refusals. It does not run Lean/Lake, certify theorem or mathematical correctness, expose proof bodies or private source refs, call providers, or authorize release/publication.
organs.wiring_note: its verifier-trace evidence cell consumes the first-wave formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop result/board/validation receipts plus the Ring2 failure-taxonomy source digest, atop the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine

organs.human_gloss: It checks whether a Lean proof cites a library result (a lemma or definition) that was never on its approved list. Even after a prover accepts a proof, that proof can still quietly use a library symbol it wasn't allowed to, and this organ surfaces those out-of-bounds uses as an inspectable record that names each symbol and where the rule came from. A cold reader should care because "the proof checked" does not mean "the proof stayed within the allowed set of building blocks," and this makes that gap visible without ever reading the proof's own steps.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it over symbol-observation rows whose qualified references are already extracted, scoring each known symbol against the allowed premise set and getting a classification: UNDECLARED_LIBRARY_PRIOR (route bridge_escalate) when a known library symbol is outside the allowed ids, or PREMISE_BUDGET_VIOLATION (route retry) which takes precedence when cited-unallowed ids are present. It also emits a secret-exclusion scan confirming no proof bodies, private source refs, oracle ids, or provider payloads entered the receipts.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only projects the symbol-boundary classification mechanic over copied non-secret Lean/Std premise rows and pre-extracted symbol observations; it does not read proof source, run Lean or Lake, prove theorem correctness, treat the whole standard library as an implicit allowlist, claim Mathlib availability, call providers, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: consumes the real Ring2 premise-index substrate and anchors the Mathlib-absent boundary on the corpus_readiness_mathlib_absence_gate and tactic_portfolio_availability_probe receipts

organs.human_gloss: When a math-proving system searches for the supporting facts (\"premises\") a proof will need, this organ replays saved records of that search and reports, problem by problem, how much of the needed support the search actually turned up. Per problem it labels one of four outcomes: the search found everything needed and the proof went through; it found everything needed but the proof still failed; it found only some of the needed support; or it found none of it. Separating \"the proof failed even though every needed premise was found\" from \"the proof failed because the search missed a needed premise\" tells you which part to fix. It also runs as a regression guard that refuses inputs which try to slip the answer into the search itself (the known-correct premises planted in the ranked results), leak proof text, tune on the test answers, or claim more than retrieval-quality numbers.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to compute per-problem precision@k / recall@k over copied Ring-2 retrieval rankings and bucket each problem into retrieval_hit / partial_retrieval_miss / retrieval_miss / proof_failure_despite_hit, plus aggregate hit-over-candidate precision and hit-over-needed recall. The validator emits blocking findings (status blocked, non-zero exit) for oracle/needed premise ids planted in rankings, leaked proof/provider/private body fields, test-split tuning flags, metric overclaims (proof/benchmark/provider/release authority), a missing adversarial-decoy case, incomplete copied-material provenance, and source-artifact digest mismatches; receipts carry import ids/digests only, never proof bodies.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: These are after-the-fact retrieval-attribution labels and precision/recall counts over copied run records only. The organ does not run Lean or Lake, call any provider, expose proof bodies, tune on test answers, claim benchmark performance, prove theorem correctness, or authorize release, and its labels are explicitly forbidden from flowing into provider context. The aggregate numbers describe only the copied fixture/bundle replayed, not any benchmark standing.
organs.wiring_note: Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas) plus digest-verified copied source artifacts; the paper module names no consuming or feeding organ.

organs.human_gloss: Replays a synthetic "did the AI fix the bug?" coding-benchmark fixture and checks that each claimed result row carries the evidence references honest grading would need - a locked grader id and config, a file-access log, a contamination check, a trusted reference score, and an output replay - and that every row maps to a declared test case. Rows missing that evidence, or that try to game or leak the benchmark (peeking at hidden answers, training on the test set, exposing the oracle patch, cherry-picking the best of many tries, or asserting a score), are flagged as quarantine cases. It works only on bundled example data with no real bug-fix or score behind it, so a cold reader can see the shape of honest benchmark evidence and the common ways such benchmarks get gamed.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm the bundled synthetic benchmark-claim fixture passes the evidence-reference, case-roster-binding, anti-gaming, and contamination-boundary floor checks and emits the expected quarantine cases plus body-free receipts - not to obtain, verify, or assert any benchmark or SWE-bench score, agent capability, or provider result.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It authorizes only body-free integrity-replay receipts and does not establish any benchmark or SWE-bench score, agent capability, provider execution, live-repo mutation, private/oracle/hidden-gold body access, product progress, or release readiness.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas) plus digest-verified copied macro-pattern provenance bodies

organs.human_gloss: This organ checks that the bundles of context an AI agent would assemble before calling an outside model provider stay inside fixed size limits (in bytes), fill their sections in the declared order until the budget runs out, list any section that was dropped for not fitting, and never carry answer keys, proof solutions, or other "correct answer" material. A cold reader can see the exact size ceilings for each recipe, which sections fit versus got left out, and which output each recipe is allowed to produce, so the context boundary is inspectable as plain accounting before any provider call or answer authority is ever in play. It only validates this metadata; it does not itself call any provider.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate that its planned provider-context recipes respect their fixed byte budgets, fill sections in declared order under those budgets, emit an omitted-sections manifest whenever something is dropped, and route to the correct deliverable type, while rejecting any recipe that authorizes a provider call or carries truth-side, oracle, or proof-body material. It also digest-checks the copied standard/source-module bodies it ships. Output is context metadata and verdicts only.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates context-budget projection mechanics (byte ceilings, ordered section fill, omitted-section manifests, deliverable routing, and digest-checked source-body imports) only. It does not authorize provider/API calls, run Lean/Lake, expose or carry proof or oracle truth-side material, assert theorem or domain correctness, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas, authority ceiling); it copies standard/source-module bodies by digest but does not feed or consume other named organs at runtime.

organs.human_gloss: This takes a small, purpose-built Lean math file (a handful of toy theorems written just for this demo) and a tiny project setup, copies them into a throwaway scratch folder, and actually tries to compile them with the Lean theorem-prover and its Lake build tool that you have installed on your machine. It then writes down exactly what happened: whether the Lean and Lake tools were found, whether the build passed, fingerprints (hashes) of the source files, the names of the theorems it defined, and how many lines each file had. It also deliberately feeds in a broken proof and a couple of off-limits files to confirm they get rejected. The point is to let a cold reader watch real proof-checking machinery run on a small example, while keeping the written records honest and redacted: no proof text or internal logs leak out, and it states plainly that this is a narrow toy check on one fixture, not a general-purpose proof system.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to get a redacted receipt confirming that a small public Lean fixture compiles under the locally installed Lean/Lake toolchain, and that the guardrails fire on the negative cases: Mathlib/Aesop/Batteries imports are rejected, private source refs and embedded proof bodies in manifests are refused, and an intentionally invalid Lean proof is rejected. The receipt carries tool-availability status, Lake build status, source hashes, declaration names, line counts, and negative-case coverage only; it never exports proof bodies, stdout/stderr text, or provider payloads.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It authorizes only a witness that a tiny declared public toy proof compiled under the locally installed Lean/Lake toolchain in a temporary workspace, plus confirmation that its leakage guardrails fired. It does not authorize Mathlib/Aesop/Batteries-dependent or general proof or theorem-program authority, provider calls, private proof import, benchmark or performance claims, whole-system correctness, or any release, hosted deployment, or publication.
organs.wiring_note: Self-contained Lean/Lake replay specimen; its manifest only cites formal-math readiness fixtures as provenance refs while it binds at runtime to the shared kernel + private-state-scan / authority-ceiling spine.

organs.human_gloss: This is the assembly point for the Lean/proof toolkit: it runs nine smaller formal-math pipeline checkers together and folds their results into one leak-proof report that labels every line by where it came from — a Lean verifier, an answer-key (oracle) comparator, an AI suggestion, a retrieval miss, or a row thrown out for breaking the rules. The report carries only references, hashes, counts, and verdicts — never the actual proof text, AI output, or answer-key bodies. A cold reader can open one receipt and immediately see which claims a Lean verifier actually backed versus which are just hints or were rejected, instead of taking a pile of separate outputs on faith.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to compose the nine public formal-math organs into one receipt and confirm the proof packet obeys its declared contract, keeping verifier-backed, oracle-compared, provider-suggested, and contract-rejected rows in separate buckets, with proof/provider/oracle bodies excluded from the receipt.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates the declared public contract shape of the proof packet and component receipts only; it does not prove anything correct, count oracle/provider output as forward proof success, import private or Mathlib-dependent proof bodies, call providers, mutate source, or claim benchmark solve rates, release, or maturity.
organs.wiring_note: composition root that runs and folds receipts from nine named formal-math organs (corpus_readiness_mathlib_absence_gate, lean_std_premise_index, formal_math_premise_retrieval, tactic_portfolio_availability_probe, target_shape_tactic_routing_gate, ring2_premise_retrieval_precision_recall_harness, formal_math_verifier_trace_repair_loop, proof_diagnostic_evidence_spine, formal_math_lean_proof_witness)

organs.human_gloss: It copies a small public Lean math project into a throwaway temporary workspace and actually runs the Lean/Lake checker on a handful of small, bounded proof-step attempts the tool builds itself. It then writes down what the checker said: which attempts were accepted, which failed, and the failure category for each, plus safety counts (for example, how many attempts tried to sneak in forbidden content and were rejected). You can read the pass/fail facts and the safety counts for yourself, while the tool never shows the underlying proof text, never calls any outside service, and never modifies the original project or your own source files.
organs.agent_gloss: Run it for a structured, leak-proof receipt of which bounded Lean transition candidates the local Lean/Lake checker (run on a temp copy of a public fixture) accepted or rejected, with residual-retry and bounded policy reruns re-checked the same way. Proof bodies, raw tactic scripts, and provider/oracle text are excluded (proof_body_exported=False, body_in_receipt=False); the receipt carries only ids, action classes, Lean return codes, accept/residual verdicts, safety counters, authority ceiling, and anti-claim.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It is a tool-witness receipt for bounded public Lean transition rows only: it does not establish general proof authority, count oracle/provider output as proof, export proof bodies or tactic scripts, call providers, mutate source, claim benchmark solve-rates, or authorize release/publication.
organs.wiring_note: Self-contained Lean-execution replay specimen over its own fixture; binds only to the shared kernel + secret-exclusion/receipts/authority spine.

organs.human_gloss: This checks that a folder's local "how to get around" surface behaves: you start from one control entry, then drill into browsable lists of routes and cards, and those browse rows are never allowed to pose as the source of truth. It makes visible, in plain receipt files, that stale or mislabeled navigation is caught, that compressed cards keep a note of what they left out, and that nothing private or secret leaks into the navigation material while moving around. It only inspects toy fixture files and exact copied-but-non-secret navigation source modules; it does not touch any live system.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm a fixture's route-plane contract holds and that browse rows stay projections, not authority. The fixture run exercises eight expected negative cases and confirms each is detected: (1) stale source coupling bundled with a banned first-contact drilldown route, (2) a route card missing its omission receipt, (3) an atlas projection claiming control-entry authority, (4) a route card leaking private body content, (5) a route summary overclaiming freshness while coupling is stale, (6) a duplicate route id, (7) entry-payload compaction dropping a required control-floor field, and (8) an affordance-passport anti-trigger row that must be demoted before similarity can select it. A separate validate-route-plane-bundle subcommand additionally checks copied source-module digests and required anchors plus a secret-exclusion scan, with all body text kept out of the receipts.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public toy route-plane contract and its regression fixtures (plus exact copied non-secret navigation source modules in the bundle path); it does not prove live route freshness, grant source authority, authorize any later organ, run any provider/live-kernel call, or certify the whole wave.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private/secret scans, receipts, schemas); its "feeds" entries are self-contained pattern assertions, not live consumption of other named organs

organs.human_gloss: Replays a fixed set of pre-recorded work-landing situations against a toy repository and shows, in the receipts it writes, when a change would be allowed to "land" versus blocked: two claims competing on the same file, a claim built on a stale parent commit, a claim missing its owned path, a clean preflight check that wrongly says the work is already finished, and which commit lane (a narrow scoped commit vs a broad checkpoint) a dirty working tree is permitted to use. A cold reader can open the resulting receipts to see exactly why each situation was permitted or refused, instead of trusting an opaque "it's done" message.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm that the projected work-landing, claim, and checkpoint-lane metadata obeys the expected rules and emits the expected blocking error codes (e.g. SAME_PATH_CLAIM_CONFLICT, EXPECTED_PARENT_MISMATCH, PREFLIGHT_PASS_OVERCLAIMS_WORK_LANDED) before treating any change as landed.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates work-landing, claim, checkpoint-lane, and dependency metadata projections over fixed fixtures only; it does not mutate live ledgers or git, certify real closeout, authorize broad staging without operator intent, or prove any change is actually correct or complete.
organs.wiring_note: consumes the shared work_landing and mission_transaction_preflight macro-tool builders plus the shared secret-exclusion/receipts/schemas spine; it does not feed or consume other named organs

organs.human_gloss: This checks recorded examples of an agent finishing a piece of work the careful way: each example must name the exact files it claims to have touched, show that validation was recorded before any commit was attempted, and only label itself "committed" if the example also records the repository's HEAD moving. It also checks that blockers and ledger closeout were captured. So a cold reader can see whether each recorded work-claim carries the required evidence and ordering, rather than being an unbacked chat boast. It judges the recorded claims against the contract; it does not run Git or prove that any commit truly landed in a real repository.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this over recorded work-landing rows to confirm they satisfy the declared contract (each row cites owned claimed paths and owner-native validation refs; validation precedes the commit attempt; a "committed-landed" claim carries a declared HEAD-before != HEAD-after advance; metadata-blocked rows capture a blocker ref; Work Ledger closeout is cited) and to confirm the fixture's nine negative rows are each rejected with their expected error codes. It validates the recorded contract only; it does not execute Git or prove real-world landedness.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public work-landing contract over recorded rows; it does not mutate Git, stage unrelated dirty paths, prove a commit actually landed, export private source bodies, call providers, publish/host, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret_exclusion_scan, receipts, schemas)

organs.human_gloss: It checks whether a claim that an AI agent "replicated a research paper" comes with the paper trail that real replication would leave behind - not by re-running anything, but by confirming the bundle names every required piece of evidence: a breakdown of the paper's contributions, a grading rubric, the list of allowed public inputs, a from-scratch repo scaffold, an experiment plan, the metric scripts, a roster of declared file-hashes for the outputs plus hashes that all stay inside that roster, a grader report, a capped compute/runtime budget, an ablation diff, a failure list, and a cold-rerun receipt. It also catches eight ways a claim can cheat - reusing the original authors' code, leaking a hidden rubric, calling a run a "success" when only a write-up backs it, asserting a benchmark score, leaking a private paper or dataset body, using unbounded compute, grading only the final answer, or pointing at a file-hash that was never declared. The work runs on two made-up sample papers (one machine-learning method, one computational-science study), and a cold reader can open the generated receipt to see which of the eight cheats each test case triggered, rather than taking "it was replicated" on trust.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to validate a replication-claim bundle: that the two synthetic paper capsules each carry all fourteen required evidence references (rubric tree, contribution decomposition, allowed-input refs, scaffold, experiment DAG, metric scripts, declared+actual artifact-hash refs, grader report, budget, ablation diff, failure taxonomy, cold-rerun receipt), that the policy forbids the prohibited shortcuts, and that all eight negative fixtures fire their expected error codes (author-code reuse, hidden-rubric leakage, report-only success, benchmark overclaim, private-body leak, unbounded compute, final-answer-only grading, undeclared artifact hash). It validates reference presence and receipt shape only; it does not execute the scripts, run a rerun, or call providers.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates the shape and presence of synthetic replay metadata and receipt references only - it does not run any experiment, metric script, or rerun, does not authorize any claim that a paper was actually replicated, that a benchmark score was achieved, or that the underlying science is correct, and it never calls providers, exposes private paper/data bodies, or authorizes publication or release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, schemas, and the shared public-agent-execution-trace macro tool); does not feed or consume another named organ

organs.human_gloss: This organ shows, in plain receipts, where a projected copy of a world model has drifted away from its real source. For each drift it names the signal, points to where the real source lives, gives a suggested repair route, and cites the test that would confirm the fix. A cold reader can see the drift is being flagged honestly: the projection never claims to be the source of truth, and the receipts deliberately leave out any private or credentialed data.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate that a set of body-free drift rows each cite a source ref, repair route, validation ref, and target ref while keeping the forbidden booleans (source-authority, live-repair, source-mutation, doctrine-promotion, provider-export, private-export, release) false, and that the secret-exclusion scan passes with no body text emitted. The organ's fixture mode additionally asserts that the eight expected negative cases (missing source ref, missing validation ref, source-authority claim, live-repair authorization, private-runtime export, provider-payload export, automatic doctrine promotion, release claim) are rejected; the validation receipt records 8/8.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only validates the declared public, body-free drift-receipt contract; it does not inspect private runtime bodies, mutate or claim authority over source, repair live routes, promote doctrine, export provider payloads, or authorize publication or release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine

organs.human_gloss: This replay takes six made-up "what if" spatial scenes from robotics and self-driving-style settings (a forklift appears from behind an occlusion, a small pedestrian steps into a crosswalk, a gust pushes a drone off course, a shiny floor fools a robot into seeing free space, a stacked load shifts into a lane, and an oncoming car turns late) and shows each one as inspectable rows: the starting scene, the action taken, the predicted next scene, what changed between them, a sanity check, and honest notes on its limits (it is synthetic, not real-world ground truth). A cold reader can see exactly what a spatial "world model" claim is built from, plus a checklist of dangerous claims it deliberately refuses to make.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to validate that the synthetic counterfactual-replay rows are well-formed (each cites scene-state, action-trace, predicted-state, transition-diff, oracle-check, and public sensor-packet refs plus limitation labels) and that every forbidden claim (private video export, raw sensor export, live robot/AV operation, real-world geographic accuracy, simulator-product claim, generated-video-only authority, benchmark scores, release) is rejected by a negative-case receipt.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public contract of synthetic spatial counterfactual-replay metadata rows; it does not operate robots or AVs, claim real-world geographic accuracy, sell or validate a simulator product, treat generated video as authority, report benchmark scores, call providers, publish, host, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, public payload boundary, receipts), pulling in a copied non-secret macro source body but feeding no other named organ

organs.human_gloss: This takes the workflow of "tracing which internal features inside a model drove an answer" and turns it into inspectable local records. Each row links feature ids to a machine-readable graph of connections, records the before/after results of poking those features (the causal-intervention deltas), notes how far the explanation can be trusted (its faithfulness limit), and points to where the underlying evidence lives. A cold reader can see that every interpretability claim is backed by checkable evidence, and that the records deliberately hold no model weights, no raw activations, no prompts, and no hidden reasoning — they carry only refs, digests, counts, and verdicts.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to check that a circuit-attribution replay bundle meets the declared public contract: each row carries feature-to-edge links, causal intervention deltas, sufficiency and faithfulness limits, a secret-exclusion scan, and target/source refs, while private or raw bodies (weights, activations, prompts, hidden reasoning) and any release/provider/benchmark authority are absent. It validates the receipt contract only; it does not inspect a live model.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public circuit-attribution runtime-receipt contract. It does not authorize model-transparency product claims, live model access, export of private weights/raw activations/proprietary prompts/hidden chain-of-thought, provider calls, benchmark scores, or publication/release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (copies non-secret macro source bodies into its own bundle rather than feeding or consuming other named organs)

organs.human_gloss: This validator takes a sample (synthetic, not live) record of an agent's local run — the route it picked, the work it did, the events it logged, the evidence it pointed to, and the authority limit it declared — and checks that this recorded trail is well-formed and self-consistent, instead of leaving you to read raw log JSON. A cold reader cares because the record is built to state, up front, where the agent's authority was supposed to stop, so the limits are written down and checkable rather than taken on faith. It checks the recorded evidence; it does not watch a live agent or prove one actually stayed in bounds.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate a synthetic route-feedback fixture against the declared public contract — checking actor-axis authority boundaries, route-lease consumption, duplicate trace ids, hook-shadow advisory status, anti-pattern debt retirement, and behavior-change evidence gates. It emits a pass/blocked receipt over recorded fixture metadata only; it does not read live operator state, provider payloads, or certify runtime behavior.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only public, recorded trace-feedback metadata and regression fixtures; it does not inspect live operator state, certify or prove runtime behavior, read provider payloads, mutate the Task Ledger, authorize pattern assimilation, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: Consumes and validates exported bundles from other named macro-tools/organs (agent_execution_trace, agent_session_attribution, continuation_packet/multi-agent fan-in, agent_trace_route_repair, agent_observability_store, bridge_resume, controller_heartbeat) via dedicated validate-*-bundle subcommands.

organs.human_gloss: Replays a small, fake (synthetic) record of a paused background job to check that the rules for safely resuming it hold. It confirms the job left behind a real resume note, that trying to resume the same job twice is refused, that "still alive" pings can never count as permission to resume (and a long-stale ping can't be passed off as proof the job is still healthy), and that only a proper close-out receipt is allowed to claim the work actually finished. It also includes deliberately broken cases to prove each rule rejects them. A cold reader can inspect these continuity rules in plain receipts without any live job, network call, or private data ever being touched.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm, against a public synthetic fixture, that detached-job continuity rules hold before relying on them: a resumable job needs a continuation packet, a packet resumes exactly once (duplicate resume is rejected), heartbeats are liveness-only (and stale heartbeats are not live-health evidence), resource pressure blocks dispatch as a recorded decision, worker-skip rows dedupe without silently closing a claim, and only a closeout transition receipt marks work as landed. Each rule is exercised by a matching negative case.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public continuity contract over synthetic fixtures; it does not run live bridge transport, call providers, read operator HUD/browser/phase-runtime or private-memory state, prove provider or UI uptime, land work, mutate source, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (receipts, private-state scan, strict-JSON schemas)

organs.human_gloss: When a piece of work lands in the local substrate, this organ checks the closeout records for it — confirming that exactly one same-lane "what did we learn from this" decision was filed per landed item, that any claimed refinement names an owner-visible surface and the artifact it changed, that a "nothing to refine" entry carries its required typed fields, and that there are no duplicate or off-lane entries. It runs over fixture data and makes the closeout bookkeeping inspectable, so a cold reader can see whether the recorded closeouts conform to the system's stated learning-from-landed-work rules, instead of taking that on faith.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate that each landed organ in the fixture set has exactly one same-lane closeout decision: either a concrete refinement receipt naming the owner surface and changed artifact, or a typed nothing_to_refine receipt with stewardship checked, next-best-lane checked, and a re-entry condition. It also checks residual lifecycle posture, rejects duplicate refinement receipt ids, rejects local-lesson authority upgrades to global doctrine, and rejects raw-seed bodies in fixtures. It asserts only that the declared closeout contract holds over fixtures, not that any live learning actually occurred.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public closeout contract over synthetic fixture data; it does not ingest private lessons, mutate live ledgers, promote global doctrine, authorize release or publication, make provider calls, claim private-data equivalence, or certify public runtime behavior.
organs.wiring_note: Reads landed-organ closeout summaries and binds only to the shared acceptance-validator + evidence/authority spine; the paper module names no organ it feeds or consumes.

organs.human_gloss: This checks the short guided tour Microcosm advertises for a first-time reader: compile a folder into local .microcosm state, follow one route through its patterns, work, events, and evidence, and land on the receipt that backs each claim. The organ does not run that tour itself; it inspects the declared tour and keeps it honest, requiring each impressive-looking count to sit next to the boundary it must not cross, so a cold reader can move from a number to a receipt to the source without trusting marketing.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm the declared public entry path clears its density floor (>=5 steps, >=4 distinct commands, >=4 distinct evidence refs), that the public claim carries every required phrase, that the evidence map rows are complete and marked projection-not-authority, that the secret-exclusion scan reports zero blocking hits, and that all four negative fixtures fire their expected codes (release/hosting overclaim, private-data-equivalence overclaim, missing evidence refs, and marketing-only material lacking runtime commands).
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public reveal walkthrough contract (steps, commands, evidence refs, evidence map, claim phrases, secret-exclusion scan, and negative-case rejection) and authorizes nothing about release, hosted deployment, publication, recipient work, provider calls, secret export, private-data equivalence, Lean/Lake execution, or whole-system correctness.
organs.wiring_note: validates a reveal path that explicitly names the `microcosm intake` runtime bridge, `formal_math_readiness_extensions`, and the observatory/macro-projection intake board, while itself consuming only fixtures and the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine

organs.human_gloss: This is the checkpoint that handles bringing material from the larger private project into the public Microcosm folder. When someone proposes a batch of files to import, it verifies each one: it only accepts non-secret material, and only when the destination file, a content fingerprint (to confirm the copy matches), a record of where it came from, and the supporting checks all line up. Anything held back as private or secret has to come with a written note saying so, and attempts to claim more authority than allowed are rejected. The point is that a cold reader can inspect exactly what was imported, what was deliberately left out, and what was refused, so the public copy stays honest about its limits instead of quietly leaking private source or pretending to be more than it is.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to preview or validate a projection bundle before import: confirm each proposed cell has source and public-target refs, a matching content digest, omission receipts for any withheld private material, a body-free receipt contract, and an authority ceiling that stays capped below release. Use `plan` for a no-write preview (emits the intake-preview schema and per-cell ready/blocked status); use `run-projection-bundle` to validate-and-record.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It authorizes only verified non-secret macro body import with provenance and content-digest checks; it does not grant source authority, private-root equivalence, release, hosted deployment, publication, recipient work, provider or Lean/Lake execution, secret or private-source-body export, or any whole-system correctness claim.
organs.wiring_note: feeds macro_body_import_floor into microcosm status/spine, bridges via microcosm intake to reveal/evidence, and coordinates per-cell import statuses across named organs (e.g. formal_math_readiness_gate, agent_route_observability_runtime, mission_transaction_work_spine)

organs.human_gloss: Runs a made-up forecasting case through the discipline a careful predictor would have to defend: which way a fork was called and why the losing side was ruled out, whether each prediction stayed inside the pre-declared list of allowed outcomes, that no "after the fact" evidence got used as if it were known in advance, how the guesses compared to a synthetic "what actually happened" result, and that any edits to the running record were small, allowed changes rather than rewrites. A cold reader sees the reasoning laid out as inspectable records instead of being handed a verdict. Everything is invented test data — it makes no real forecast and claims no track record.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to check that a synthetic prediction packet passes the projection gates (resolved CP1 fork with kept invalidation rationale, CP2 targets inside the declared universe, no post-target evidence refs, graded oracle diff, bounded public-delta dossier edits), that the six negative fixtures each trip their expected rejection code, and that the authority ceiling keeps trading/advice/provider/live-market/publication/release/private-equivalence all false.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It exercises projection mechanics on a synthetic, invented packet only. It does not establish forecasting correctness or accuracy, give trading/financial/investment advice, call live market data or providers, publish predictions, claim any performance or track record, import private data, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret_exclusion_scan, receipts, schemas); it projects a CP1/CP2/oracle/dossier pattern group but does not feed or consume other named organs

organs.human_gloss: This is a coverage checker that reads the project's public catalogs and confirms every accepted part is still tied to a written standard, a documented way to run it, and a saved proof-of-run, while flagging any claim that overreaches (like "ready to release") or any leaked private text. A cold reader should care because it makes the system's own bookkeeping inspectable: you can see at a glance whether the pieces are accounted for instead of taking that on trust.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to confirm that every accepted organ in the public inventory maps to a standard, a runtime contract (CLI command plus runtime step), and at least one receipt ref, and that the diagnostic policy and inputs carry no release/provider overclaim and no private-source or provider-payload body.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public coverage contract and never becomes source authority for the registries, mutates source, exposes private material, or authorizes release, provider calls, or any whole-system-correctness claim.
organs.wiring_note: Reads the shared public registries/inventory as projection inputs and binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine; it does not feed or invoke other organs.

organs.human_gloss: It checks Microcosm's "what do I run first" guided path so that every step on the cold-reader route map is backed by a real command, a public doc reference, and an evidence receipt instead of just prose promises. A newcomer can therefore trust that the suggested ten-minute first-run tour is actually wired and honestly labeled, with small verified counts shown as plain accounting rather than success badges.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm the declared first-run route sequence starts with the front-door commands (tour, status card, proof lab) and is ordinal-sorted, that each route names a runnable command plus a public docs reference and resolves by route_id to at least one receipt ref in the route-receipts file, and that nothing carries release/provider overclaims or private source bodies.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It is projection-only metadata that validates the declared public route contract; it is not route-registry authority and authorizes no source mutation, provider calls, release/publication, financial advice, private-data equivalence, or whole-system correctness.
organs.wiring_note: consumes the first-screen card as a handoff and sequences/binds named sibling organs (tour, status, authority, workingness, explain, observe, compile) by requiring each route row to point at their commands and receipts

organs.human_gloss: It replays three made-up agent trajectories (a normal one, one trying to hide a failure, and one overstepping a tool's permissions) and checks that every monitor verdict — including a clean "pass" — comes with the receipts needed to re-run and verify it. A cold reader can see exactly which checks each verdict depends on, and that any verdict missing its backing evidence gets quarantined instead of trusted. It is a demo over fictional data, not a measurement of how well a real monitor catches anything.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to confirm that monitor verdicts (pass/escalate/block) and coverage labels in a synthetic bundle are receipt-backed (each requires suspicious-span, adversarial-probe, escalation, body-omission, mitigation, and cold-replay refs) and that forbidden inputs (private reasoning, internal code, exploit detail, credentials, live traffic, performance claims, coverage-without-probe) are correctly rejected as falsification fixtures.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: This is a synthetic, body-free replay smoke check only; it does not run live agents, call providers, claim monitor product performance or control-eval scores, export private reasoning/code/exploit/credential material, import live traffic, mutate source, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, authority_ceiling)

organs.human_gloss: This inspects whether a write-up that claims an AI agent was caught secretly working a hidden side-goal actually carries the supporting structure a reader would need: the step-by-step actions, a per-step "how suspicious did the monitor find this?" score, and a stated counterfactual saying that removing the sneaky steps would drop the hidden goal while keeping the real task working. It runs only on fake, scrubbed example data, so it shows what the skeleton of such a safety claim should look like and checks the pieces line up with each other. It does not run a real agent, does not detect real scheming, and proves nothing about how any deployed monitor performs.
organs.agent_gloss: Run this to check that a synthetic sabotage/scheming-monitor claim carries the required body-free evidence shape (episodes, action traces, per-step monitor scores, a declared counterfactual replay, and cold-replay rows) and that the pieces are internally consistent: the macro builder recomputes each episode's caught verdict from the monitor suspicion scores and re-derives whether the counterfactual isolates the side task, flagging any mismatch. It also confirms all seven forbidden negative cases (live sabotage, real credentials, exploit detail, private chain-of-thought, raw harmful payload, final-grading-only, deployment scare claim) are rejected with the expected error codes. Pure fixture-shape validation over synthetic data — not real scheming detection, no agent execution, no provider calls.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: A body-free synthetic smoke/projection demo of the replay-claim shape and its internal consistency only. It does not authorize or perform live agent execution or live sabotage, and makes no claim about exploit instructions, credentials or account material, private reasoning, harmful payloads, monitor product performance, deployment risk, benchmark scores, provider calls, source mutation, or release. It is not behavioral or safety validation and is not a measure of any real monitor's detection ability.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas) plus the macro_tools.agent_execution_trace recompute helper; no other named organ feeds or consumes it

organs.human_gloss: Replays a canned three-step story: an agent's memory first records two facts (a preference and a tool result), then learns one is now out of date and edits the preference and deletes the stale fact, then re-runs the same task once with memory on and once with memory off. It lets a cold reader check, from plain receipts, that the update and deletion each carry a "conflict" and "downgrade" receipt before the memory is allowed to affect the answer, that the memory-on vs memory-off runs are compared through logged evidence rather than just the final wording, and that private-thread content stays in the record only as metadata pointers, never as copied text. It checks the bookkeeping of this synthetic example; it does not judge whether the agent's memory decisions were the right ones.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this against a synthetic fixture to see the shape of the memory-update bookkeeping it should produce: updates and deletes of older memory must carry temporal conflict-edge and stale-downgrade refs before they may affect a replay; private threads appear only as metadata-only refs with no exported body; and a memory-on replay must cite evidence handles while the memory-on/off pair is reconciled by an answer-delta receipt, not by final-answer comparison alone. It also confirms seven failure fixtures (raw transcript export, private-candidate auto-promotion, stale-preference override, memory-as-source-authority, vector recall without an evidence handle, final-answer-only credit, active-injection authority) are all rejected. It validates structure and receipt presence on fixture rows, not the correctness of any real memory system.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates the projection mechanics of a synthetic memory fixture only — that the required refs, decisions, paired replays, negative cases, and secret-exclusion scan line up and that receipts are body-free. It does not claim live-memory product quality, judge whether memory decisions were domain-correct, treat memory recall as source authority, adopt active injection, export private transcripts, call providers, mutate source, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, schemas, and a public execution-trace builder), not to other named organs

organs.human_gloss: This is a worked, made-up example of how an agent should handle a tampered "memory": a poisoned note gets spotted, held in quarantine, blocked from being used in any later action, and finally deleted-with-an-audit, with a clean re-run recorded showing the bad memory is no longer there. The organ does not actually delete anything or perform the re-run; it reads the on-disk record of that whole guard-and-cleanup sequence and checks that every required step and receipt lines up, so a cold reader can see exactly which checks must hold. It works entirely from synthetic refs and metadata, with no private memory or transcripts exposed.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to check that a synthetic memory-poisoning handling record has all the required pieces (provenance-bound write proposals, a quarantine verdict on the untrusted poisoned source, a retrieval-blocked-before-action gate, and a rollback with a deletion-audit ref plus a cold-rerun receipt marking the memory absent), that a body-free private-state scan finds no leaks, and that all eight known bad patterns (e.g. private-body export, live-user-memory claim, trusted promotion from untrusted context, deletion without audit) are each rejected with the expected error codes.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only checks the structural shape and internal consistency of a synthetic memory-security policy projection recorded as JSON. It does not run or validate any real memory store, does not itself quarantine, delete, or re-run anything, and does not prove that any system actually resists poisoning. It exports no private memory bodies or transcripts, calls no providers, mutates no source, produces no benchmark scores, and authorizes no release (all authority-ceiling flags are hardcoded false).
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas, and the shared forbidden-classes policy)

organs.human_gloss: When an AI agent uses outside tools (look something up, change a ticket, read a result from an untrusted source), this checks a recorded log of that tool use to confirm each action was properly fenced: bound to a narrow permission, approved before it changed anything, given a way to undo it, and kept from letting untrusted tool output boss the agent around. It runs on a small built-in make-believe example that carries only labels and references (no real accounts, secrets, or tool contents), so a cold reader can see the safety checks an agent's tool use is supposed to pass without anything touching a live account.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to check that a recorded tool-use bundle is admissible: every call has a narrow scope ref, writes carry approval plus ledger plus rollback refs, untrusted output is treated as data not instruction, cold-replay receipts exist, payloads/account refs stay metadata-only, and all eight expected negative-abuse cases fire. It validates presence and consistency of this evidence only; it does not run tools, hit providers, or authorize anything.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only checks that the tool-authority evidence in a recorded bundle (scopes, approvals, rollbacks, instruction/data splits, cold replays, redaction, and the expected abuse-case failures) is present and internally consistent. It does not run tools or authorize live MCP/account access, credential or payload export, treating tool output as instruction, source mutation, benchmark safety scores, or release, and it makes no claim that the underlying tool-use policy is domain-correct.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts, and the shared public agent-execution-trace macro tool); no other named organ is fed or consumed

organs.human_gloss: Replays a make-believe example of "should this change be allowed to run?" and shows, step by step, why each proposed action was permitted. All three actions (a look-only inspection, a small config write, and an undo of that write) had to carry proof evidence and two visible policy approvals before anything was admitted; on top of that, the two actions that actually change something (the config write and the undo) also had to show a logged record of the change and a matching undo receipt. A cold reader can see that just holding a password or credential is never treated as permission, and that nothing here touches a real account or makes any real change.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm a mutation-authorization claim is backed by replayable proof, pre-execution visible verdicts, logged-and-reversible side effects, and that the eight rigged failure cases (standing credential, policy-after-execution, hidden vote, live cloud credential, irreversible mutation, unlogged side effect, consensus-without-evidence, final-answer-only) are all caught.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only a declared, synthetic governed-mutation contract and authorizes no live cloud/account action, standing credentials, source or irreversible mutation, policy-after-execution, hidden votes, provider calls, benchmark-score claims, or release.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private_state_scan, receipts, schemas); bundle mode also imports copied non-secret macro bodies, not other named organs

organs.human_gloss: Takes a recorded, synthetic bundle of agent steps on three partially-observable toy tasks (a terminal investigation, a mock purchase, a small planner) and checks that every "the agent did the right step" reward is actually backed by a checkable verifier or observed feedback reference, not by hidden reasoning, formatting tricks, a smuggled answer key, or a final-answer-only score. It does not run or watch a live agent; it validates pre-recorded files. A cold reader can open the resulting receipt files and see, per step, the belief summary, the reward, and whether the reward-hacking and replay checks passed, so it is inspectable why each reward was or was not allowed.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate a recorded belief-state/process-reward fixture bundle and confirm that its seven planted failure cases are each caught: hidden chain-of-thought export, neural-judge-only labels, hidden gold labels, reward-by-formatting, verifier bypass, benchmark-performance claims, and final-answer-only scoring. It also runs a secret-exclusion scan and emits redacted receipts (result, board, validation, acceptance).
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It only checks that the projection's accounting lines up under its own schema rules over recorded synthetic fixtures; it does not authorize hidden-reasoning export, RL training, hidden gold or neural-judge-only labels, benchmark-performance claims, provider calls, source mutation, or release, and proves nothing about real-world reward, live agent behavior, or domain correctness.
organs.wiring_note: Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (receipts, secret-exclusion scan, schemas) plus the shared agent-execution-trace macro tool; it does not feed or consume other named organs.

organs.human_gloss: This takes a synthetic record of an agent attempting risky actions inside a sandbox and lays out, step by step, what each action requested, whether a safety policy approved or blocked it before it would have run, what (if anything) changed afterward, and for the actions that did run, whether the change was rolled back and could be re-checked. A cold reader can see exactly how each containment decision is recorded: every blocked attempt is still logged as a traced step but is marked as never executed with no resulting change, all from local files with no real secrets, network, or live agent involved.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate that a sandbox-policy fixture has pre-execution policy verdicts on every one of the six action requests, body-free side-effect/rollback/cold-replay receipts (4 blocked with zero side effects, 2 executed with verified rollback), a public agent-execution trace span per request, and that the eight escape negative cases (real_secret_material, live_network_access, raw_environment_export, policy_after_execution, unlogged_side_effect, tool_output_policy_bypass, executable_escape_payload, security_benchmark_claim) all trip. Validation covers projection/trace-refactor mechanics over a synthetic fixture only.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates the projection / trace-refactor mechanics over a synthetic fixture only; it authorizes no live sandbox escape, secret or credential handling, live network access, host filesystem mutation, executable payload export, raw environment export, provider calls, security benchmark claims, source mutation, or release. A pass proves the projection boundary and trace-refactor mechanics for this contract, not real sandbox security, exploit resistance, or whole-system safety.
organs.wiring_note: Binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine, reusing the macro_tools agent_execution_trace builder; it does not feed or consume other named organs.

organs.human_gloss: Replays a recorded sample agent episode (built from synthetic, body-free rows) and makes visible whether the instructions the agent treated as trusted were kept separate from untrusted web, tool, or browser text before any sensitive action was taken. A cold reader can see, row by row, where untrusted text flowed, what the policy decided for each flow (allow / warn / block / review) before the action, and that the recorded outcome leaked no secret and disclosed no trusted context. It also bundles deliberately-bad cases it must reject (e.g. untrusted text reaching a sensitive action ungated, or a credential being exfiltrated).
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to confirm that, in this replayed synthetic episode, every recorded flow from untrusted source text to a privileged action was gated (blocked, sent to review, or sanitized) before the action, and that the recorded outputs disclosed no trusted context or credential. Evidence is the validated fixture rows plus a body-free agent-execution trace, not a live agent run or a general robustness guarantee.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: Passing receipts only show this projection satisfies the named information-flow contract over synthetic, redacted, body-free rows; they do not prove general prompt-injection robustness, benchmark performance, live account/tool/provider safety, hidden-message handling in a real system, source-mutation authority, or release authority.
organs.wiring_note: binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (secret-exclusion scan, receipts) plus an internal agent_execution_trace macro refactor, not other organs

organs.human_gloss: Takes a claim that an AI agent "found and fixed a security bug" and lays it out as a local, inspectable chain of made-up (synthetic) evidence: the imagined target, the suspected issue, the trace pointed to as backing, a reference to an abstract exploitability argument, the patch, and regression tests the fixture says fail before the fix and pass after it. The organ checks only that these pieces are all present, refer to each other consistently, and carry no real targets, exploits, payloads, credentials, or attack steps; it does not run the tests or judge whether the bug or fix is actually real. A cold reader can open the receipt and see whether the declared chain holds together, with no real attack material ever present.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to check that a vulnerability-discovery-and-patch claim carries a structurally complete, body-free, secret-free evidence chain (target to hypothesis to trace to abstract-exploitability ref to patch to declared-passing regression tests to sandbox verdict to cold replay) with every stage present and cross-referenced, before treating the claim as inspectable. It validates declared structure and the secret/live-access exclusion only; it does not execute tests, exploit anything, or judge real-world correctness.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the projection/evidence-chain mechanics of a synthetic replay: structural presence, cross-reference consistency, declared boolean flags, and the secret/live-access exclusion scan. It does not execute the regression tests and makes no judgment of real-world security or fix correctness. It authorizes no live-target testing, real CVE exploitation, weaponized payloads, credential handling, network exfiltration, actionable exploit steps, provider calls, source mutation, benchmark security scores, release, or any whole-system security claim.
organs.wiring_note: Self-contained replay specimen; consumes the shared public agent-execution-trace refactor (build_public_agentic_vulnerability_patch_proof_trace) and binds to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine, not to other named organs.

organs.human_gloss: Takes the pattern of a "self-driving materials lab" (propose a candidate material, run safety screens, simulate an assay, then decide what to try next) and replays it locally as inspectable records: every step, its safety gate, its simulated result, and the decision that followed, plus the pre-recorded points where such a loop would fail and where it would restart.
