It makes the workflow's structure visible, all on a simulator-only fixture, so a cold reader can trace how the loop is wired without any real lab, real chemicals, or real robot ever being involved.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to validate that a closed-loop materials-lab replay fixture is body-free and simulator-only, and to confirm its safety gates reject wetlab protocols, hazardous synthesis steps, reagent amounts, controlled/bioactive targets, live lab credentials, robot commands, private lab notebooks, and discovery claims.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It documents projection and replay mechanics only and does not authorize wetlab protocols, hazardous synthesis steps, reagent amounts, controlled/bioactive targets, robot commands, live assay data, discovery claims, benchmark scores, provider calls, or any judgment of domain/chemical correctness.
organs.wiring_note: Refactors the shared lab_evolve_replay macro tool and binds only to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine (private-state scan, receipts, schemas); no other named organ is fed or consumed.

organs.human_gloss: It builds a small public Lean/Lake project, then runs the Lean verifier over declared "transition" rows that reference a set of generated "certificate" declarations, and writes a structured receipt showing which rows the verifier accepted, which it left unresolved or rejected, plus the exact build command, return code, and file hashes. A cold reader sees honest, inspectable evidence that a real Lean verifier ran on public material, with proof text, provider/oracle output, and private source deliberately excluded from the receipt and that exclusion recorded (not silently dropped) rather than passed off as evidence.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs it to get a structured pass/blocked receipt confirming a small public Lean/Lake fixture compiled in a temporary workspace copy and that declared transition rows were accepted, rejected, or left residual by the local Lean verifier, with oracle/provider sidecars carried only as comparison rows (never counted as proof), and no proof bodies, provider calls, or source mutation.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It is a local tool-witness that the declared public fixture rows compiled and were adjudicated by the local Lean verifier; it does not authorize general proof authority, count oracle/provider output as proof, expose proof text, mutate source, claim a benchmark solve-rate, or authorize release.
organs.wiring_note: Self-contained Lean replay/validation specimen (CP2 and Evolve are internal reruns over its own certificate rows) that binds only to the shared secret-exclusion scan, receipt writer, and authority-ceiling spine.

organs.human_gloss: It makes the system's "learn from a lesson, then improve" cycle inspectable on a folder. Each local lesson is shown being assigned to a specific owner surface (a skill, a doctrine page, a standard, or a tracked to-do capture), then changed or captured there, then validated, then closed out with a concrete reason to revisit later. A cold reader can confirm, using local files only, that improvements are tied to real owner-surface changes and evidence rather than just asserted.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to check that each declared lesson legitimately refined a named owner surface (with validation) or was captured with a re-entry condition, while raw operator voice, private thread bodies, provider payloads, direct doctrine-node edits, and unvalidated global-promotion claims are rejected by negative cases.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public-safe contract of the loop on fixtures; it does not export raw operator voice or private bodies, grant source/doctrine mutation, global-promotion, live Task Ledger mutation, or publication authority, make provider calls, prove correctness, or claim private-root equivalence.
organs.wiring_note: Imports macro loop shapes (recursive self-improvement, doctrine population, local-to-general propagation, Task Ledger) only by public-safe source refs/hashes; binds to the shared kernel + evidence/authority spine, not to other live organs.

organs.human_gloss: This is a checker for the system's catalogue of named thinking-moves (operators like "reduce competing pressure to one bounded action" or "compile a handoff packet when validated work cannot be committed"). It confirms every operator in the public catalogue is fully described (how it is triggered, used, and checked) and that every operator marked active carries a real receipt proving it once changed a live decision, while rejecting any operator that claims to speak with the owner's voice or overreaches into "ready to release." A cold reader should care because it shows the system treats reasoning itself as inspectable typed substrate instead of ad-hoc prompt lore.
organs.agent_gloss: An agent runs this to confirm every operator row in the public cognitive-operator registry carries the required operator-shape fields (activation, process, integration, validation, evidence, dogfood receipts), that each active operator has a dogfood receipt with cognition_delta_evidence, that near-duplicate operators carry an accretion decision (anti-sprawl), and that no row claims operator-voice authority, leaks a private body, or sets a release/provider/mutation overclaim.
organs.claim_ceiling_restated: It validates only the declared public registry contract and copied non-secret macro bodies; it never becomes registry source authority, mutates operators, proves operator correctness, exposes raw operator voice, or authorizes release, provider calls, or any whole-system-correctness claim.
organs.wiring_note: Reads the public cognitive-operator registry, standard, and dogfood inputs plus the copied macro bodies as projection inputs, binding only to the shared kernel and evidence/authority spine; it does not feed or invoke other organs.
